solutions.solution: One line is added from problem figures (0) to (b), (b) to (d) and (d) to the last figure.
One line is added and deleted alternatively and all the lines move half side and one side alternatively anti-clockwise after rotating 90°.
One arrow is added in each of the subsequent figures and all pre-existing arrows are reversed.
Figure rotates 90° clockwise in each of the subsequent figures.
The shaded circle moves 1, 2, 3, 4, ... steps clockwise in each of the subsequent figures.
L-shaped figure rotates 90°, 45°, 90°, ... clockwise and is reversed in each figure.
In each of the subsequent figures, the larger symbol becomes small and a new figure, bigger in size, comes before it.
One arrow is added in clockwise direction and other arrows are reversed.
In first step, the small figure becomes larger and a new figure, small in size, comes inside it. In second step, the outside figure is removed. The process continues.
Upper triangle rotates 90° anti-clockwise, middle triangle rotates 45° after every two figures and the triangle rotates 90°, 180° clockwise alternatively in each of the successive figures.
One arrow in clockwise direction is added in such a way that direction of the head of arrow is same as that of other arrows and at the same time all pre-existing arrows are inverted.

First and second signs, counting from clockwise direction interchange positions and move one side anti-clockwise. The sign, out of these two, which comes first is replaced by a new sign. The other sign moves one side anti-clockwise in the successive figures.
The leaf rotates by 90° anti-clockwise and one shaded leaf and blank is added alternatively in each of the successive figures.
Every square rotates 90° clockwise one by one in each of the subsequent figures.
